metadata.description: Seed data for Maiat — AI agent trust/review platform. Public data only.
metadata.note: Market data snapshot as of 2026-02-23. Prices change rapidly.

agents.name: Hey Anon
agents.description: HeyAnon is an AI DeFi protocol that simplifies on-chain interactions by executing swaps, bridging assets, managing DeFi strategies, and aggregating project information — all via natural language prompts.

trust_signals.open_source_notes: Integration guide and token list open on GitHub; core backend not fully open
trust_signals.audit_details: Regular audits mentioned in community; specific firm/report not publicly linked
trust_signals.team_notes: Built by RealWagmi, a semi-anonymous team with prior track record in DeFi
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: active — swaps, bridging, staking live; 266K+ tokens staked

agents.review_seed: HeyAnon, built by the RealWagmi team, offers a genuinely functional DeFi execution layer supporting swaps, bridging, and strategy automation across multiple chains via a clean natural-language interface. The project has an open integration guide on GitHub, mentions regular audits, and has been live since November 2024 without known security incidents. Trust is moderately constrained by the pseudo-anonymous team and the lack of a publicly linked audit report, though the OFT V2 multi-chain token standard adds a layer of technical credibility.

agents.name: Brian AI
agents.description: Brian is an AI-powered Web3 intent engine that allows users and AI agents to interact with DeFi through natural language prompts — executing swaps, bridges, smart contract generation, and protocol research non-custodially via API.
core_functions: LangChain AI agent SDK for on-chain actions

contract_addresses.notes: No native token. Infrastructure/API product only.

trust_signals.open_source_notes: 13+ public repos on GitHub including langchain-toolkit, SDKs
trust_signals.audit_details: No public audit report found; operates as API layer, not a smart contract protocol
trust_signals.team_notes: Team appears to be Italy-based; LinkedIn presence at BrianknowsAI; no token means lower rug risk
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: API-based; non-custodial — user signs all transactions themselves

market_data.notes: No native token. Revenue model is API access / Prompt-to-Earn program.

agents.review_seed: Brian AI is one of the most technically credible DeFi execution agents in the space: fully non-custodial (users sign all transactions), open-source SDKs on GitHub, operational since 2023, and no native token removes most speculative risk. The absence of a public smart contract audit is partially mitigated by the fact that Brian acts as an intent layer — it builds transactions but never holds user funds. High trust relative to peers.

agents.name: Ethy AI
agents.description: Ethy AI is an autonomous trading assistant built on the Virtuals Protocol (Base chain). It enables users to define DeFi strategies in plain language, monitors markets, and executes trades autonomously. Notable for handling over $171M in ACP (Agent Commerce Protocol) volume on Virtuals.

trust_signals.open_source_notes: No public GitHub found; closed-source agent on Virtuals
trust_signals.audit_details: No dedicated audit; inherits Virtuals Protocol smart contract security
trust_signals.team_notes: Creator anonymous via Virtuals launchpad; no team publicly identified
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: $171M+ processed via X402/ACP protocol; ~50% of Virtuals agentic GDP at peak

agents.review_seed: Ethy AI demonstrates real on-chain traction with $171M+ in autonomous trading volume through Virtuals' Agent Commerce Protocol, making it one of the most active execution agents by usage metrics. However, its anonymous team, closed-source codebase, and sub-$2M market cap warrant caution — the project's longevity depends heavily on continued integration with and investment from the Virtuals Protocol ecosystem. Trust is moderate: strong execution metrics, weak transparency.

agents.name: Wayfinder
agents.description: Wayfinder is an omnichain AI agent protocol developed by Parallel Studios. It enables AI agents ('Shells') to execute blockchain tasks via natural language across Ethereum, Solana, and Base. Built to power the Colony game and broader on-chain agent routing infrastructure.
core_functions: DeFi swaps and smart contract interactions

contract_addresses.notes: PROMPT is an ERC-20 token on Ethereum. OKX listing April 2025.

trust_signals.open_source_notes: No public GitHub for Wayfinder core; Parallel Studios is the backer
trust_signals.audit_details: No public audit found; Parallel Studios has raised multiple VC rounds
trust_signals.team_notes: Built by Parallel Studios (Canada-based); team has track record from Parallel TCG card game and VC-backed rounds in 2021 and 2024
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: Closed alpha stage as of early 2025; live but limited public access

community.notes: Twitter @AIWayfinder active; linked to Parallel gaming community

agents.review_seed: Wayfinder benefits from the credibility of Parallel Studios, a VC-backed gaming company with a demonstrated track record — this makes it one of the more institutionally backed AI agent projects in the field. The omnichain routing vision is technically ambitious and addresses a real need. However, the product remains in limited access (closed alpha), the team is partially anonymous relative to VC standards, and the token has dropped 91% from ATH, raising questions about execution timeline.

agents.name: Pippin
agents.description: Pippin is a Solana-based autonomous AI agent that originated from an accidental experiment by Yohei Nakajima (BabyAGI creator) in late 2024. The agent manages its own wallet, creates on-chain content, deploys tokens, and evolves via modular skills — functioning as a 'digital being' rather than a traditional DeFi bot.

trust_signals.open_source_notes: Framework fully open source as 'Digital Being Framework for Autonomous Agents'
trust_signals.audit_details: No smart contract audit; Pump.fun token with no pre-mine at launch
trust_signals.team_notes: Creator Yohei Nakajima (BabyAGI founder) is well-known in AI/Web3 space; token was community-driven, no pre-mine
trust_signals.supply_warning: CoinGecko/Bubblemap: ~80% of supply reportedly controlled by interconnected insider wallets — significant concentration risk
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: Active; autonomous wallet transactions ongoing on Solana

community.notes: No official Twitter; community-driven project; Yohei Nakajima (@yoheinakajima) is the primary public face

agents.review_seed: Pippin's $738M market cap is extraordinary for what began as an accidental community experiment, and its open-source Digital Being Framework and doxxed creator (Yohei Nakajima, BabyAGI) provide genuine legitimacy. However, a critical red flag from Bubblemap indicates ~80% of supply is controlled by interconnected insider wallets — a severe concentration risk that undermines the fair-launch narrative. Approach with caution despite the compelling AI-native framing.

agents.name: Griffain
agents.description: Griffain is a Solana-based AI agent platform that coordinates specialized autonomous agents for trading (snipe, DCA, flipper), NFT minting, wallet management, and on-chain research. It has processed 1M+ automated transactions since launching in late 2024.

contract_addresses.notes: GRIFFAIN token on Solana; supported by Solana Labs

trust_signals.open_source_notes: No public GitHub found for core platform
trust_signals.team_notes: Team maintains some anonymity per Solana Compass; backed/supported by Solana Labs
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: 1M+ automated transactions since launch; active ecosystem with Agent Baxus and other specialized agents

agents.review_seed: Griffain demonstrates strong product traction with 1M+ automated transactions and a dedicated ecosystem of specialized agents on Solana, supported by Solana Labs validation. However, the semi-anonymous team, lack of public audit, and closed-source platform are concerns for a product that executes live trades. The 98.75% drawdown from ATH may reflect post-hype correction rather than fundamental failure, but dilligent users should monitor for execution risk before committing significant funds.

agents.name: AIXBT
agents.description: AIXBT is an AI-driven crypto market intelligence agent launched via Virtuals Protocol. It monitors 400+ crypto KOLs on social media to detect emerging narratives and deliver alpha insights. Often called 'the Bloomberg of crypto' — token holders get access to the agent's private terminal and analysis.

contract_addresses.notes: Tokenized AI agent on Virtuals Protocol (Base chain)

trust_signals.open_source_notes: Closed-source AI agent; operates via Virtuals Protocol
trust_signals.audit_details: No dedicated audit; inherits Virtuals Protocol smart contract security
trust_signals.team_notes: Created by pseudonymous Virtuals user 'Rxbt' (maintainer @aixbt_labs); no doxxed founders
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: Active signal feed; 283K token holders; token holder terminal with exclusive alpha

agents.review_seed: AIXBT achieved remarkable traction — 472K Twitter followers and 283K token holders in under 3 months — validating strong market demand for AI-powered crypto alpha delivery. Its signal quality has reportedly shown edge in anticipating price movements, and the Virtuals Protocol infrastructure provides baseline smart contract security. The main risks are the fully pseudonymous creator, the agent's heavy dependence on social data (susceptible to manipulation), and the 98% drawdown from ATH that suggests significant speculative excess in early pricing.

agents.name: Zerebro
agents.description: Zerebro is an AI-native crypto protocol on Solana that uses autonomous agents to generate content (text, art, music), interact across social platforms, create NFTs, and participate in on-chain trading. Built with RAG and high-entropy data to avoid model collapse. Notable for launching the open-source ZerePy framework.
core_functions: autonomous content creation (text, art, music, NFTs)

contract_addresses.notes: Native token on Solana launched via Pump.fun; multi-chain interactions

trust_signals.open_source_notes: ZerePy framework is open source and used by other projects
trust_signals.audit_details: No formal audit; Pump.fun origin token with no pre-mine at launch
trust_signals.team_notes: Co-founder Jeffy Yu (22) controversially died by suicide in May 2025 during a livestream; death was initially suspected to be faked to exit liquidity; community remains deeply uncertain about project continuity and team intent

known_incidents.description: Co-founder Jeffy Yu reportedly died by suicide on May 4, 2025, during a livestream. On-chain sleuths initially suspected a faked death to exit liquidity; obituary posted; situation remains controversial and unresolved.

trust_signals.on_chain_activity: Token active; ZerePy framework in use; primary AI agent operations uncertain post-founder incident

community.notes: @0xzerebro is the AI agent's own account with limited followers; broader Zerebro community via blorm.xyz

agents.review_seed: Zerebro was a groundbreaking autonomous AI agent experiment and the ZerePy open-source framework remains a meaningful technical contribution to the space. However, the project carries an extraordinary red flag: co-founder Jeffy Yu's May 2025 death (during a livestream) — surrounded by on-chain evidence of potential exit liquidity and unresolved community controversy — makes this one of the highest-risk profiles in the AI agent ecosystem. Treat with extreme caution; do thorough independent research before any engagement.

agents.name: Virtuals Protocol
agents.description: Virtuals Protocol is the leading AI agent generator platform on Ethereum's Base L2 (launched October 2024). It enables anyone to create, tokenize, and monetize AI agents with revenue-sharing tokenomics. AIXBT, Ethy AI, and thousands of other agents were launched on Virtuals. The GAME framework powers agent functionality.

contract_addresses.notes: VIRTUAL ERC-20 on Base; 30+ public repos on GitHub

trust_signals.open_source_notes: 30 public repositories on GitHub including protocol contracts
trust_signals.audit_details: Code4rena public audit (April 2025); ongoing security focus
trust_signals.team_notes: Founded by Jansen Teng and Weekee Tiew; publicly identified; team background in gaming and AI
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: Thousands of AI agents launched; largest AI agent ecosystem on Base; hit $1B+ market cap by Dec 2024

agents.review_seed: Virtuals Protocol is the most credible AI agent launch platform in the current cycle: doxxed founders, a Code4rena audit, 30+ open-source repos, and the largest active AI agent ecosystem on Base. Its deflationary tokenomics and agent commerce protocol (ACP) show systemic design thinking beyond pure speculation. The primary risks are ecosystem-level — if the AI agent narrative fades, VIRTUAL (down 88% from ATH) could continue declining — but as infrastructure, it has the most robust trust signals of any agent platform reviewed.

agents.name: Autonolas (Olas)
agents.description: Autonolas (now rebranded Olas) is the most mature AI agent infrastructure protocol in Web3, founded in 2021. It provides an open-source framework (Open Autonomy) for building autonomous multi-agent services, with on-chain registries, Gnosis Safe multi-sig execution, and staking incentives. Best known for Olas-powered prediction market trading agents on Gnosis Chain.
core_functions: on-chain agent registries (services, components as NFTs)
multi-agent service coordination via Gnosis Safe

contract_addresses.notes: OLAS ERC-20 on Ethereum; extensive on-chain registry contracts across multiple chains

trust_signals.open_source_notes: Fully open source; all agent framework code public; Valory AG is the core dev entity
trust_signals.audit_details: Multiple security audits; protocol has been audited as infrastructure; ongoing since 2021
trust_signals.team_notes: CEO David Minarsch publicly identified; Valory AG team doxxed; academic/research background
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: 3M+ autonomous agent transactions; Olas agents are top users of Gnosis Chain

community.notes: @autonolas on X; active Discord and developer community; Pearl app for end users

agents.review_seed: Autonolas (Olas) is the most trustworthy protocol in this dataset by traditional standards: doxxed founders, fully open-source infrastructure, multiple audits, and the longest track record (operational since 2021). The 3M+ autonomous transactions on Gnosis Chain and prediction market integrations demonstrate real utility, not speculation. The 99.5% drawdown from ATH and very low current market cap ($9M) relative to the infrastructure quality suggests severe market dislocation — this is either a deep value opportunity or a signal of limited current demand for institutional-grade AI agent infrastructure.

agents.name: CreatorBid
agents.description: CreatorBid is an AI creator economy hub for launching, growing, and monetizing AI agents on-chain and across social platforms (X, Telegram). It uses sniper-proof fair-launch smart contracts and curated launches. Since October 2024, 3,500+ AI agents have been launched on the platform. Based on Base blockchain.
core_functions: AI agent launchpad (rapid deployment in under 1 minute)
AI content generation for X and Telegram

contract_addresses.notes: BID token on Base; ecosystem agents on Base and BNB Chain

trust_signals.audit_details: No public audit found; 'sniper-proof fair launch contracts' claimed but unverified
trust_signals.team_notes: No doxxed founders or team found publicly
trust_signals.on_chain_activity: 3,500+ agents launched; ecosystem active on Base

community.notes: Platform active on Base; Discord/Telegram size not found

agents.review_seed: CreatorBid has achieved real traction with 3,500+ agent launches and an active creator economy on Base, positioning it as a meaningful alternative to Virtuals Protocol for AI agent deployment. However, the anonymous team, absence of a public audit, closed-source platform, and small market cap ($3.3M, down 96.7% from ATH) raise meaningful trust concerns. The 'sniper-proof fair launch' claims are positive but need independent verification — use with caution until team transparency improves.
